ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wimu no ɔsor. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebien nye no.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.
translated: And God created mankind in his own image, Yes, in the image of God he created mankind; he created them male and female.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Hwɛ, mama hom nduadzewa na nduaba a ɔwɔ asaase nyinara enyi, dɛ ɔnyɛ edziban mma hom:
translated: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat:

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara yɛ.
translated: And he created the heavens and the earth and all that is in them.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hyiraa da a otsia esuon no do na ɔtseew ho: osiandɛ ɔno mu na ɔhomee fii no ndwuma a ɔayɛ nyinara ho. Eden Ture
translated: And God blessed the seventh day and made it holy: because on it he rested from all the work he had done. Genesis

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.

translated: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: na papa nye bɔn ho nyimdzee dua no dze, mma nndzi bi: na da ara a ibedzi bi no, ampaara dɛ ibowu.
translated: and father is not ashamed of the town, let him not say anything: and the day that you will say it, truly you will die.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: w’enyim mfifir mu na ibedzidzi kɛpem dɛ ebɛsan akɔ daadze; osiandɛ ɔno mu na woyii wo fii: na huntuma nye wo, na huntuma mu na ebɛsan akɔ.
translated: your face will be darkened and you will be ashamed and go back home; because from there you were born: and dust you are, and to dust you shall return.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.

translated: but Cain and his offering did not please him. So Cain was very angry, and his face fell.

text: Na Kain see EWURADZE dɛ, Mo bɔn ho akatua so kyɛn dɛ mubotum asoa.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.

translated: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every bird of the heavens; upon everything that creeps on the ground, and all the fish of the sea, they are delivered into your hand.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.

translated: And Abram said to Lot, Please let there be no strife between me and you, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen; for we are brethren.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”

translated: And God saw it in his heart, that, yes, I know that your heart's intention in this is good, and you did not think evil in yourselves to sin against me: that is why I did not allow it to happen.

text: Na Abimelek bisaa Abraham dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ihunii, ma ayɛ dɛm adze yi?

translated: And when the woman whom God gave me from my father's house, the one I married, when I married that woman, this is the kindness I ask of you; wherever we arrive, say of me that, He is my brother.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Aman nsom wo, Na amanaman nkotow wo: Yɛ wo nuanom do wura, Na wo na mba nkotow wo: Ndom nka hɔn a wɔdom wo nyinara, Na nhyira nka hɔn a wohyira wo nyinara. Esau Bisa Nhyira
translated: Let nations serve you, And peoples bow down to you: Be lord over your brothers, And let your mother's sons bow down to you: Cursed be those who curse you, And blessed be those who bless you. Esau Asks for a Blessing

text: Na, hwɛ, EWURADZE gyina n’etsifi, na ɔsee dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE, w’egya Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, na Isaak ne Nyankopɔn: asaase a eda do no, medze mebɛma ɔwo, nye w’asefo.
translated: And, behold, the LORD stood above it, and said, I am the LORD God of thy father Abraham, and the God of Isaac: the land whereon thou liest, to thee will I give it, and to thy seed.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Na w’asefo bɛyɛ dɛ asaase do huntuma, na ebɛtserɛw akɔ anee, boka, nye kwaamu, nye pomu: na ɔwo nye w’asefo mu na wobehyira asaase do ebusua ebusua nyinara.
translated: And your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and you will spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south: and in you and in your descendants shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.

translated: And Jacob asked them, "My brothers, where are you from?" And they answered, "We are from Haran."

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”

translated: And he asked them, "Is he well?" And they said, "He is well; and behold, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep."

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”

translated: And she conceived again, and bore a son: and she said, Now will I praise the LORD: therefore she called his name Judah; which means; Praise and she left bearing.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,

translated: And Rachel's maid Bilhah conceived again, and bore Jacob a second son.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.

translated: And when Leah saw that she had stopped bearing children, she took her maid Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as a wife.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.

translated: And it came to pass when Rachel had given birth to Joseph, that Jacob said to Laban, Send me away that I may go to my place and my country.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Na Jakob paa nguambaa no na ɔmaa nguan no hɔn enyim kɔr Laban nguan no mu nsensanee nsensanee nye etuntum no nyinara hɔn do; na ɔyɛɛ narankasa no nguankuw wɔ nkyɛn, na ɔammfa ammfora Laban no nguan no mu.
translated: And Jacob separated the lambs and made the sheep face toward the striped and all the dark ones in Laban's flock; and he made separate flocks for himself, and did not put them with Laban's sheep.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Na homara nyim dɛ m’ahoɔdzen nyinara na medze asom hom egya.
translated: And all my strength and all my abilities that I use to serve you all.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me. Now leave this land at once and go back to your native land.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?
translated: And Jacob became angry, and he quarreled with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have so hotly pursued after me?

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Sɛ m’egya ne Nyankopɔn, Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m’ekyir a, nkyɛ ndɛ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankopɔn ehu m’amandzehu nye me nsaano edwumayɛ, na anadwe yi ɔaka w’enyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, you would surely have sent me away empty-handed. But God has seen my hardship and the labor of my hands, and last night he rebuked you. Jacob and Laban Make a Covenant

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Na Jakob faa ɔbo, na ɔdze yɛɛ idum sii hɔ.

translated: He said, "These belong to your servant Jacob; they are a gift being sent to my lord Esau: and behold, he himself is also behind us."

translated: And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel: for you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

translated: And when Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp," and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.

translated: And he left Jacob alone; and a certain man wrestled with him there until daybreak.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel: for you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.

translated: So Jacob became very rich, and he had many servants, and sheep, goats, cattle and donkeys were divided into two groups;

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Nna ma memfa nyimpa a wɔka mo ho yi bi mingya wo. Na ɔsee dɛ, Ma ɔnyɛ dɛn? Wɔmma minnya mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam.
translated: And Esau said, Father, let me not take any of these people who are with you. And he said, What shall I do? Let me find favor in my lord's sight.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Na Jakob sii mu kɔr Sukɔt, na okesii dan maa noho, na osisii mpata maa no mbowa: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Sukɔt.
translated: And Jacob went to Succoth, and he built a house for himself, and made booths for his cattle: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Na ɔtɔɔ asaase kakraba a ɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ do no, wɔ Sekem n’egya Hamor ne mba no hɔ, maa dwetɛ ɔha.
translated: And he bought the piece of land where he had pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for a hundred pieces of silver.

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: Mbarimba yi wɔnye hɛn tse no asomdwee mu; dɛm ntsi hom mma wɔntsena asaase yi do, na wondzi mu gua; na, hwɛ asaase yi dɔɔ so ara yie ma hɔn; hom mma yɛmfa hɔn mbabaa nwar hɔn, na wɔmma hɛn so yɛmfa hɛn mbabaa mma hɔn.
translated: Let us dwell with them in the land in peace; therefore let them dwell in the land, and trade therein; for, behold, the land is large enough for them; let us take their daughters to us for wives, and let us give them our daughters.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.

text: Na Nyankopɔn hun kan no dɛ oye: na Nyankopɔn tseew kan no fii sum no ho.

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na Nyankopɔn frɛɛ wimu no ɔsor. Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebien nye no.
translated: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Na adze sae na adze kyee, da a otsia ebiasa nye no.
translated: When a fly follows a corpse, the day it gets buried is its day too.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.
translated: And God created mankind in his own image, Yes, in the image of God he created mankind; he created them male and female.

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.

translated: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat:

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Na wowiee sor nye asaase nye dza ɔwɔ mu nyinara yɛ.
translated: And he created the heavens and the earth and all that is in it.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.

translated: And God blessed the seventh day and made it holy: because on it he rested from all the work he had done. Genesis

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,

translated: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: osiandɛ Nyankopɔn nyim dɛ da a hom bedzi bi no, hom enyi bobue, na hom ayɛ dɛ Nyankopɔn na hom ehu papa nye bɔn.
translated: because God knows that the day you eat of it, your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God knowing good and evil.

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Na EWURADZE Nyankopɔn bisaa ɔbaa no dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ayɛ yi? Na ɔbaa no see dɛ, Abowatsena no na ɔdaadaa me ma midzii.

translated: I hate the one who hates your daughter, and your family is my family: she gives birth to you, and you also become her support.

translated: your face is dark in it and you will be ashamed and go back; because from it you came out: for dust you are, and to dust you shall return.

translated: And Cain knew his wife: and she conceived, and bore Enoch: and he built a city, and called the name of the city Enoch, after the name of his son.

text: na ebubur no ennya ne nan sibew, na ɔsanee baa no hɔ wɔ hɛn no mu, osiandɛ nsu no wɔ asaase nyinara enyi: na ɔtseen ne nsa suoo no mu, na ɔdze no baa ne nkyɛn wɔ hɛn no mu.
translated: and the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot, and she returned unto him into the ark, for the waters were on the face of the whole earth: then he put forth his hand, and took her, and pulled her in unto him into the ark.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Na EWURADZE tsee afɔr no no huam; na EWURADZE kaa wɔ n’akoma mu dɛ, Monnkɔdom asaase bio ara da wɔ nyimpa ntsi, osiandɛ minyim dɛ nyimpa n’akoma mu nsusui muo dze fitsi no mbofra mu; nkyii so mennkɛsɛɛ adze a nkwa wɔ mu biara bio ara da, dɛ mbrɛ mayɛ yi.
translated: And the LORD smelled the sweet savour; and the LORD said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man's sake; for the imagination of man's heart is evil from his youth; neither will I again smite any more every thing living, as I have done.

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Na hom ho suro nye hom ho hu mbra asaase do mbowadoma nyinara nye wimu ntumbowa nyinara do; dza wɔwea daadze nyinara, na po mu nam nyinara, wɔdze ahyɛ hom nsa.
translated: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every bird of the heavens; upon everything that creeps on the ground, and all the fish of the sea, they are delivered into your hand.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Na nam a no bɔgyaa da ho wɔ no mu dze, mma hom nndzi.
translated: And if there is blood in the meat, do not eat it.

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Na Nyankopɔn kasaa kyerɛɛ Noa, nye ne mba a wɔka no ho no dɛ,
translated: And God spoke to Noah, and his sons who were with him, saying,

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,

translated: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: na mebɛkaa m’ahyɛmu a ɔda emi nye hom nye honam nyinara mu abɔdze a nkwa wɔ mu nyinara ntamu; na nsu no nnkɛyɛ nsuyirii bio asɛɛ honam nyinara.
translated: and I will establish my covenant which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Na Noa no wenyin no fii n’enyi do, na ɔtsee dza ne ba kuma no dze ayɛ no.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Na Abram nyaa no ho kɛsenara wɔ fiembowa, nye dwetɛ, nye sika mu.
translated: And Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Na otuu no kwan fi Pomu afamu araa kodur Bɛtɛl, beebi mbrɛ ahyɛse no n’asesee wɔ mu no, wɔ Bɛtɛl nye Ai ntamu:
translated: And he went on his journey from the south as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai:

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Na Abram see Lot dɛ, Meserɛ wo, mma emma aperper mmba emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mo mbowahwɛfo nye wo mbowahwɛfo ntamu; osiandɛ yɛyɛ enuanom.
translated: And Abram said to Lot, Please let there be no strife between me and you, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen; for we are brethren.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.

translated: Then Lot chose all the plain of Jordan; and Lot journeyed east: and they separated themselves the one from the other.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,

translated: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot settled among the cities of the plain and pitched his tent near Sodom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Afei Sodomfo yɛ ehuhufo nye ndzebɔnyɛfo kɛsenara tsia EWURADZE.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.

translated: And I will give to your descendants all the land of the earth: and if anyone tramples on the land of the earth, he will not be able to trample on your descendants.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Na afe a otsia duanan no mu na Kedorlaomer na ahemfo a wɔka no ho no bae, na wobokum Refafo wɔ Asterot-karnaim, nye Zuzfo wɔ Ham, nye Emfo wɔ Savɛ-kiriataim,
translated: And in the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings that were with him came and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-karnaim, and the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-kiriathaim,

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.

translated: And they took Lot, Abram's nephew who lived in Sodom, and his possessions, and went away.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: gyedɛ dza mberantsɛ no edzi no nko, na Aner, Eskol nye Mamre a wɔnye me kɔree no hɔn kyɛpɛn.
translated: Let the young men only eat what they have consumed, and the portion of the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre—let them take their share.

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Na Abram see dɛ, EWURADZE Nyankopɔn, ebɛnadze na edze bɛma me, hwɛ dɛ minnyi ba na menam, na nyia obedzi m’adze nye Eliezer a ofi Damaskɔs anaa?
translated: And Abram said, LORD God, what will you give me, seeing I go childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Na ɔsee no dɛ, Emi nye EWURADZE a medze wo fi Kaldeafo Ur wɔ Kaldea bae, dɛ medze asaase yi rebɛma wo, ma edze aayɛ egyapadze.
translated: And He said to him, I am the LORD who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess it.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Na Sarai see Abram dɛ, Afei hwɛ, EWURADZE esiw me awoo kwan; meserɛ wo, kɔ m’afonabaa nkyɛn; annyɛ a mibenya no ho mba. Na Abram tsiee Sarai ne ndze.
translated: And Sarai said to Abram, Now behold, the LORD has prevented me from bearing children; I pray you, go in to my maidservant; it may be that I will obtain children by her. And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.

translated: And when Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years, Abram and his wife Sarai took her maidservant Hagar, the Egyptian, and gave her to Abram as a wife.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Na ɔkɔr Hagar nkyɛn, na onyinsenii: na aber a ohun dɛ onyinsen no, obuu no wuraba enyimtsia.
translated: And he went to Hagar, and she conceived: and when she saw that she had conceived, her mistress was despised in her eyes.

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Na Abram see Sarai dɛ, Hwɛ, w’afonabaa wɔ wo nsamu, yɛ no dɛ mbrɛ woara ehwɛ a oye no ara. Na Sarai nye no dzii no dzendzenndzen, ma ofii n’enyim guanee.
translated: And Abram said to Sarai, Behold, your maidservant is in your hand, do to her as it pleases you. And Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she fled from her presence.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Na EWURADZE no bɔfoe hun no wɔ nsunyiwa bi ho wɔ sar do, Sur kwan mu nsunyiwa no ho a.
translated: And the angel of the LORD found her by a fountain of water in the wilderness, by the fountain in the way to Shur.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.

translated: Now look, I have two daughters who have not known a man: please let me bring them out to you, and do to them as is good in your eyes: only to these men do nothing; for they have come under the shadow of my roof.

translated: And God saw it in his heart, that, yes, I know that your heart's intention is good in this, and you did not also think in yourself to commit sin against me: that is why I did not allow it to happen.

text: Na Abimelek bisaa Abraham dɛ, Ebɛnadze na ihunii, ma ayɛ dɛm adze yi?

text: Na Abraham see dɛ, Me ba, Nyankopɔn noara bɛkyerɛ eguambaa a wɔdze no bɔbɔ ɔhyew afɔr: na hɔn beenu nyina bɔɔ mu kɔree.

translated: Look, I am standing by this well; and let it be that when a young woman comes to draw water and I say to her, Please, give me a little water from your jar to drink;

translated: And I asked her, Whose daughter are you? And she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor's son, whom Milcah bore unto him: and I put the earring upon her face, and the bracelets upon her hands.

text: Na wodzidzii na wɔnomee, ɔno nye mbanyin a wɔka no ho no, na wotsintsiim hɔ anafua no nyinara. Na wɔsoɛɛr anapa no, ɔsee dɛ, Hom ngya me kwan ma monkɔ mo wura hɔ.
translated: And when they arrived at their destination, it was the elder they had mentioned, and they greeted him there that whole evening. And when morning came, he said, Please give me way to go to my master's place.

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Na EWURADZE see no dɛ, Aman ebien na wɔwɔ wo yamu, Na nkorɔfo ahorow ebien na wɔbɛpaa hɔnho mu efi wo yafun mu; Na ɔman no mu kor bɛyɛ dzen asen kor; Na ɔpanyin no bɔsom ekumaba no.
translated: And the LORD said unto her, Two nations are in thy womb, and two manner of people shall be separated from thy bowels; and the one people shall be stronger than the other people; and the elder shall serve the younger.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.

translated: And Jacob said to his father, I am Esau your firstborn; I have done as you told me: please arise, sit up and eat some of this meat, so that your soul may bless me.

translated: And he said, Bring it near to me, and let me eat of my son's venison, that my soul may bless thee. And he brought it near to him, and he did eat: and he brought him wine, and he drank.

translated: Let nations serve you, And peoples bow down to you: Be lord over your brothers, And let your mother's sons bow down to you: Cursed be those who curse you, And blessed be those who bless you. Esau Asks for a Blessing

translated: And Esau said, Is he not rightly named Jacob? for he has supplanted me these two times: he took away my birthright; and, behold, now he has taken away my blessing. And he said, Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

translated: And your descendants will be like the dust of the earth, and you will spread abroad to the west, and to the east, and to the north, and to the south: and through you and your descendants all the families of the earth will be blessed.

text: Na Jakob bɔɔ anohoba see dɛ, Sɛ Nyankopɔn dzi m’ekyir, na ɔhwɛ mo do wɔ ɔkwan yi a murutu yi mu, na ɔma me edziban ma midzi, nye atar ma mehyɛ,

translated: And he took all that he had and fled; and he rose up, and crossed the River, and set his face toward the mountain of Gilead. Laban Pursues Jacob

text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?
translated: And Jacob was very angry, and he quarreled with Laban: and Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have so hotly pursued after me?

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Sɛ m’egya ne Nyankopɔn, Abraham ne Nyankopɔn, nye Isaak no Suro, enndzi m’ekyir a, nkyɛ ndɛ egya me kwan nsapan. Nyankopɔn ehu m’amandzehu nye me nsaano edwumayɛ, na anadwe yi ɔaka w’enyim. Jakob Na Laban Dzi Ahyɛmu
translated: If the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, you would surely have sent me away empty-handed. But God has seen my hardship and the labor of my hands, and last night he rebuked you. Jacob and Laban Make a Covenant

translated: And he commanded the first one, saying, When Esau your brother meets you and asks you, saying, Whose servant are you, where are you going, and whose are these before you?

text: Nna Jakob n’akoma tuu kɛsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkorɔfo a wɔnye no nam no, nye mpɔnkye, nguan, anantwi nye efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn mu ekuw ebien;
translated: Then Jacob became very afraid, and was distressed. So he divided the people who were with him, and the sheep, goats, cattle and camels into two groups;

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Na esee me dɛ, Ampaara mebɛyɛ wo yie, na mama w’asefo ayɛ dɛ mpoano anhwea a no dodow ntsi wɔkan a ɔnnyɛ.
translated: And as for me, I swear I will do you good, and make your descendants as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ dza odzi kan no dɛ, Sɛ mo nua Esau nye wo hyia, na obisa wo dɛ, Woana n’abofra nye wo, erokɔ henfa, na dza wodzi w’enyim yi woana dze a?
translated: And he commanded the first one, saying, When Esau your brother meets you and asks you, saying, Whose servant are you, where are you going, and whose are these before you?

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: Na ɔkaa Jakob no nko; na banyin bi nye no dzii aper wɔ hɔ ara ma okodur adzekyee.
translated: And he left Jacob alone; and a certain man wrestled with him there until daybreak.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Wɔnnkɛfrɛ wo dzin Jakob bio, na mbom, Israel: osiandɛ ɔwo enye Nyankopɔn na ɔnye nyimpa ako, na edzi konyim.
translated: And he said, Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel: for you have struggled with God and with men, and have prevailed.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.
translated: And when Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp," and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Na Jakob tuu abɔfo ma wodzii n’enyim kan kɔr no nua Esau hɔ wɔ Seir asaase do, Edom haban mu.
translated: And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the country of Edom.

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Nna Jakob n’akoma tuu kɛsenara, na no ho hiahiaa no. Na obuee nkorɔfo a wɔnye no nam no, nye mpɔnkye, nguan, anantwi nye efumpɔnkɔ no hɔn mu ekuw ebien;
translated: Then Jacob became very afraid, and it troubled him. So he divided the people who were with him, and the sheep, goats, cattle and camels into two groups;

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Meserɛ wo, gye me fi mo nua Esau nsamu: na musuro no, bɛyɛ na ɔaba eboku emi, nye enanom nye hɔn mba yi.
translated: I beg you, deliver me from the hand of my brother Esau: for I fear him, lest he come and attack me, the mothers and the children.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Dɛm ntsi ɔdze bodu ndɛ da yi ara Israelmba nndzi ntsin a ɔwɔ ndwonku mu no: osiandɛ ɔkaa Jakob no ndwonku wɔ mbrɛ ntsin no wɔ no.
translated: The number of the children of Israel who ate the manna for forty years: because he fed Jacob's children with manna for forty years.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Meserɛ wo, wɔmma mo wura mpa ho nkedzi n’akowaa enyim: na emi mobɔdwe m’enyi ara manantsew dɛ mbrɛ mbowa a wodzi m’enyim yi botum, na dɛ mbrɛ mbofra no botum, ekodu dɛ mubodu mo wura nkyɛn wɔ Seir.
translated: I am sending messengers to your lord to find favor in his eyes: and I your servant have been staying with Laban and have remained there until now, and I have oxen and donkeys and flocks and male servants and female servants, and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your sight when I come to you my lord in Seir.

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”
text: Na Esau see dɛ, Nna ma memfa nyimpa a wɔka mo ho yi bi mingya wo. Na ɔsee dɛ, Ma ɔnyɛ dɛn? Wɔmma minnya mo wura n’enyim enyimnyam.
translated: And Esau said, Father, let me not take any of these people who are with you. And he said, What shall I do? Let me find favor in my lord's sight.

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem
text: Na Jakob sii mu kɔr Sukɔt, na okesii dan maa noho, na osisii mpata maa no mbowa: dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Sukɔt.
translated: And Jacob went to Succoth, and he built a house for himself, and made shelters for his livestock: therefore the name of the place was called Succoth.

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Na ɔtɔɔ asaase kakraba a ɔbɔɔ n’asesee wɔ do no, wɔ Sekem n’egya Hamor ne mba no hɔ, maa dwetɛ ɔha.
translated: And he bought the parcel of land where he had pitched his tent from the children of Hamor, Shechem's father, for a hundred pieces of silver.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Na Dina, Leah na Jakob hɔn babaa no, puei dɛ ɔrokɔhwɛ asaase no do mbabaa.
translated: And Dinah, Leah and Jacob's daughter, went out to see the daughters of the land.

ref: Jacob's sons wanted to get even with Shechem and his father because of what had happened to their sister.
text: Na Jakob ne mbabanyin no buaa Sekem nye n’egya Hamor ndaadaa mu, na ɔnam dɛ oegu hɔn akyerɛbaa Dina ho fi ntsi.
translated: And Jacob and his sons answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.

ref: These people really are friendly. Why not let them move freely about until they find the property they want? There's enough land here for them and for us. Then our families can marry into theirs, and theirs can marry into ours.
text: Mbarimba yi wɔnye hɛn tse no asomdwee mu; dɛm ntsi hom mma wɔntsena asaase yi do, na wondzi mu gua; na, hwɛ asaase yi dɔɔ so ara yie ma hɔn; hom mma yɛmfa hɔn mbabaa nwar hɔn, na wɔmma hɛn so yɛmfa hɛn mbabaa mma hɔn.
translated: Let us dwell with them in the land peacefully; therefore let them dwell in the land, and trade therein; for, behold, the land is large enough for them; let us take their daughters for wives, and let us give them our daughters.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Wɔfaa hɔn nguan nye hɔn anantwi, nye hɔn nsoasopɔnkɔ nye dza ɔwɔ kurow no mu, na dza ɔwɔ haban mu;
translated: They took their sheep and their cattle, and their donkeys and what was in the town, and what was in the field;

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: Asaase a medze memaa Abraham nye Isaak no, ɔwo na medze mebɛma wo, na w’asefo a wodzi w’ekyir no na medze asaase no bɛma hɔn.
translated: The land which I gave to Abraham and Isaac, that is what I will give to you, and your descendants who will come after you, I will give the land to them.

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ne kra rifi no mu no , ɔtoo no dzin Ben-oni; na n’egya dze ɔfrɛɛ no Benjamin.
translated: And when the woman's soul was departing (for she died), she named him Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.

ref: Esau and his three wives had five sons while in Canaan. Adah's son was Eliphaz; Basemath's son was Reuel; Oholibamah's three sons were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: na Oholibama woo Jeus, Jalam, nye Kora: woyinom nye Esau ne mba a wɔwoo hɔn wɔ Keenan asaase do no.
translated: and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah: these are the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Na woyinom nye Esau ne yer Oholibama ne mba; ekunyin Jeus, Jalam, na okunyin Kora. Woyinom nye ekunyin a wofi Esau ne yer Oholibama, Ana ne babaa no no mu puei.
translated: And these are the sons of Esau and his wife Oholibamah; chief Jeush, Jaalam, and chief Korah. These are the chiefs that came from Esau's wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.

ref: All of these clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom.
text: Woyinom nye Esau a ɔnoara nye Edom no n’asefo, na woyinom nye hɔn ekunyin. Seir Asefo (
translated: These are the descendants of Esau, who is also called Edom, and these are their chiefs. The descendants of Seir (

ref: Dishon's sons were Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
text: Na woyinom nye Dison ne mba; Hemdan, Esban, Itran nye Keran.
translated: And these are the sons of Dishon; Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran and Cheran.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom; ekunyin Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, na Ana,
translated: The sons of Seir the Horite were these: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

ref: which showed that Joseph was his favorite son, and so Joseph's brothers hated him and would not be friendly to him.
text: Na no nuanom no hun dɛ hɔn egya dɔ no sen hɔn nyinara no wɔtaan no, na woenntum annkasa abodwee do annkyerɛ no.
translated: And because their father loved him more than all of them, they hated him and could not speak peacefully to him.

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Na no nuanom enyi beer no do; na n’egya dze ɔdze asɛm no too ne tsirmu. Israel Soma Josef Kɔ No Nuanom Nkyɛn
translated: And his brothers were jealous of him; but his father kept the matter in mind. Israel Sent Joseph To His Brothers

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Na no nuanom kɔree kɔhwɛɛ hɔn egya no nguan wɔ Sekem.
translated: And his brothers went to pasture their father's flock in Shechem.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Na Israel see Josef dɛ, Ana wo nuanom nnyɛn mbowa wɔ Sekem bi a? Bra ma monsoma wo hɔn hɔ. Na ɔsee no dɛ, Emi nye yi.
translated: And Israel saw Joseph and said, "Are your brothers not tending the flock in Shechem? Come, let me send you to them." And he said to him, "Here I am."

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Na wɔsesee hɔnho dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔdaasofo no na ɔreba no.
translated: And when they saw him from afar, they said, Behold, the dreamer is coming.

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Na ɔhwɛe, na ɔsee dɛ, Me ba n’atar a; abowa bɔn bi na ɔakyer no; tsetsew na wɔatsetsew Josef no mu.
translated: And he looked, and he said, It is my son's coat; an evil beast has devoured him; Joseph is without doubt torn in pieces.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Na Juda hun Keenannyi bi a ne dzin dze Sua ne babaa bi wɔ hɔ; na ɔwaar no, na ɔnye no dae.
translated: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua and he married her, and he went in to her.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: Na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo babanyin; na ɔfrɛɛ no Er.
translated: And she conceived, and bore a son; and she called him Er.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Na Juda see Onan dɛ, Kɔ wo nua ne yer nkyɛn, na fa no kunabaa, na wo mba ma wo nua.
translated: And Judah said to Onan, Go in to your brother's wife, and marry her, and raise up seed to your brother.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Na Juda dze apɔnkyeba no maa ne nyɛnko Adullamnyi no, dɛ ɔmfa nkɛgye ahobadze no wɔ ɔbaa no nsa mu: naaso oennhu no.
translated: And Judah sent the young goat by his friend the Adullamite to receive the pledge from the woman's hand, but he did not find her.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ aber a ɔrowo no, nna kor dze ne nsa puei; na awogyefo no faa ahoma memen dze kyekyeer ne nsa dɛ, Oyi na odzii kan puei.
translated: And when the woman was in labor and giving birth, one put out his hand; and the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his hand, saying, This one came out first.

ref: At once his hand went back in, and the other child was born first. The woman then said, “What an opening you've made for yourself!” So they named the baby Perez.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, ɔtwee ne nsa kɔdɔɔ mu, na, hwɛ, no nua puei; na ɔwogyefo no see dɛ, ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na atsew mu aba yi? Dɛm ntsi wɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Perez.
translated: And when she was in labor, she put out her hand, and behold, his brother came out; and the midwife said, why have you broken through? Therefore they called his name Perez.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Na n’ekyir no na nyia ahoma memen kyekyer ne nsa no bae: na wɔfrɛɛ no Zera.
translated: And afterward his brother came out with the scarlet thread on his hand: and he was called Zerah.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: Na no wura hun dɛ EWURADZE nye no nam, na EWURADZE maa dza ɔyɛɛ no nyinara yɛɛ prɔmprɔm wɔ ne nsamu.
translated: And the man believed what the LORD gave him, and the LORD made all that he did to prosper in his hands.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: obiara nnyɛ kɛse nnkyɛn me wɔ fie ha; nkyii so ɔnnkam me biribiara ka wo ho, osiandɛ eyɛ ne yer: na ɔbɛyɛ dɛn na mebɛyɛ emumuyɛ kɛse yi, na mayɛ bɔn etsia Nyankopɔn?
translated: no one is greater than me in this house; neither has he kept back anything from me but you, because you are his wife: how then can I do this great wickedness, and sin thus against God?

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ, daa daa nyinara ɔkaa kyerɛɛ Josef, naaso ɔanngye no pen, dɛ ɔnye no bɛda, anaa ɔnye no bɛyɛ biribi.
translated: And it happened that day after day she spoke to Joseph, but he did not listen to her to lie with her or to be with her.

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: Na ɔkaa no dɛmara kyerɛɛ no dɛ, Akowaa Hebriwnyi a edze no brɛɛ hɛn no, ɔbaa me nkyɛn dɛ orubogu mo ho fi
translated: And she told him the same thing, saying, "That Hebrew woman whom you brought to us, she came to me to mock you"

ref: he put him in charge of the other prisoners and of everything that was done in the jail.
text: Na efiadzehwɛfo no dze adaduafo a wɔwɔ efiadze hɔ no nyinara hyɛɛ Josef ne nsa; na biribiara a wɔyɛɛ no wɔ hɔ no, ɔnoara na ɔhwɛe.
translated: And the prison warden put all the prisoners who were in the prison under Joseph's care; and whatever was done there, he was the one who supervised it.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Na hɔn beenu nyina sosoo daa, anafua da kor a ɔsor obiara ne daa, nye ne nkyerɛasee.
translated: And both of them always slept together, and every morning when they woke up, it was new and different.

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: na nkorbata ebiasa wɔ wenyindua no ho: na ɔyɛ dɛ ɔafefɛw, na ɔabɔ nhyiren; na no do nsiansiaw asow wenyinamba a ɔaber:
translated: and there were no spots on our body: and it was beautiful and smooth; and it had become more beautiful than our sister who was older:

ref: I held the king's cup and squeezed the grapes into it, then I gave the cup to the king.”
text: na Farao no kɔɔpow kitsa me nsamu, na metsetseew wenyinamba no, na mikyii guu Farao no kɔɔpow mu, na medze kɔɔpow no hyɛɛ Farao ne nsa.
translated: and Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and pressed them into Pharaoh's cup, and I placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Na sɛ inya beebi si a, ɔno kaa me, na yɛ me ayamuyie, meserɛ wo, na bɔ me dzin kyerɛ Farao, na beyi me fi dan yi mu:
translated: And if you find favor, remember me and show me kindness, I beg you, and mention my name to Pharaoh, and get me out of this house:

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three baskets are three days,
text: Na Josef buae na ɔsee dɛ, Ne nkyerɛase nye yi: nkɛntsɛn ebiasa no yɛ ndaansa:
translated: And Joseph answered him and said, This is the interpretation thereof: the three branches are three days:

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Na ɔbaa dɛ ne ndaansa no a ɔyɛ Farao n’awoda no, ɔtoo pon maa no nkowaa nyinara: na oyii nsahyɛfo panyin no nye paanootofo panyin no fii efiadze dze hɔn baa no nkowaa no enyim.
translated: And it came to pass on the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, that he made a feast for all his servants: and he lifted up the head of the chief butler and the head of the chief baker among his servants.

ref: and had the cook put to death. Everything happened just as Joseph had said it would,
text: na paanootofo panyin no dze, ɔsɛɛn no: dɛ mbrɛ Josef kyerɛɛ hɔn ase no ara.
translated: and the elders of Pharaoh's household said, it is as Joseph interpreted to them.

ref: The king went back to sleep and had another dream. This time seven full heads of grain were growing on a single stalk.
text: Na ɔdae na ɔsoo daa bio: na ohun dɛ, eburow mbɛtsem esuon efifir eburonan kor do, amaama na oye.
translated: And he lay down and slept again: and he dreamed, and behold, seven ears of corn came up upon one stalk, full and good.
